prompt_text: Close vs Pipedrive for early-stage startups
response:
# Close vs Pipedrive: Early-Stage Comparison

## **Close**
**Best if you want:** Simplicity + phone/email built-in

| Pros | Cons |
|------|------|
| Built-in calling & SMS (no extra tools) | Higher learning curve initially |
| Better for inside sales teams | Less affordable at scale |
| Strong automation workflows | Smaller marketplace/integrations |
| Transparent, simple pricing | Fewer reporting options |

**Pricing:** ~$29-99/user/month  
**Sweet spot:** Teams doing high-volume outreach (10-20 person sales teams)

---

## **Pipedrive**
**Best if you want:** Visual simplicity + flexibility

| Pros | Cons |
|------|------|
| Intuitive kanban board (easiest to adopt) | Calling/SMS costs extra |
| Great free plan for 1-2 people | Requires more integrations |
| Excellent value at scale | Mobile app less feature-rich |
| Strong marketplace of apps | Can get complex quickly |

**Pricing:** Free ($0), then ~$14-99/user/month  
**Sweet spot:** 1-5 person teams or those wanting visual pipeline management

---

## **Quick Decision Matrix**

| Factor | Winner |
|--------|--------|
| Fastest to implement | Pipedrive |
| Best free plan | Pipedrive |
| Inside sales focus | Close |
| Budget-conscious | Pipedrive |
| Phone-heavy process | Close |
| Mobile-first team | Close |

**My take for early-stage:** Start with **Pipedrive's free plan**. It's impossible to mess up and you'll know in 2 weeks if you need it.

What's your primary sales motion (outbound calls, email sequences, meetings)?
